bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I will anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s second offer is actually worse for my utility than their first, so I do not read meaningful concession from them; I will only concede on low-weight issueD, where they have consistently proposed valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still very early (t=0.04), so there is little need for major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers give me low utility and are not clearly conceding, but their latest bid included issueC:valueA, so I can make a modest concession there while holding firm on the highly important issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.93 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is little need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have not shown a clear concession trend and have recently decreased in utility to me, so I am holding firm on the three high-weight issues while conceding only on low-weight issueD.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers have not shown a clear concession trend in my favor. Their bids often include issueD:valueC, which is only a small concession for me on the lowest-weight issue, so I keep my top values on the three important issues while offering that likely-compatible value on issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have been low and erratic for my utility rather than steadily conceding. Since issueD has the lowest weight for me, I am conceding there while holding firm on my highest-value choices for issueA, issueB, and issueC. There are no opponent messages to weigh, and all prior offers used valid issues and values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.12 and the opponent’s offers to me remain low and non-conceding, mostly around 0.35–0.45 utility. I am holding firm on the high-weight issues issueA, issueB, and issueC, while conceding only on low-weight issueD where the cost to me is relatively small.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on issueC and issueD rather than dropping on the high-weight issueA or issueB. The opponent’s offers remain low utility to me and do not show a clear concession trend, though they often use issueD:valueC and have once offered issueC:valueA, so I am testing that tradeoff while keeping my core preferences intact.

At t=0.16 it is still early, and the opponent’s offers remain low for my utility with no clear upward concession pattern, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.916 rather than making major concessions. I am keeping my strongest positions on issueA and issueB, while offering issueC=valueA, which the opponent has previously proposed and still gives me solid value.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent’s offers remain low for me without a clear upward concession trend. Their bids often use issueD:valueC, and issueD is my lowest-weight issue, so I concede there while holding firm on the three high-importance issues.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 while making limited concessions from my maximum on issueC and issueD. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and do not show a clear improving trend, so I will hold firm on my most important top values for issueA and issueB while offering values they have previously included on issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.22 and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; their latest repeated offer is only about 0.266 utility to me. I will hold firm on the three high-weight issues, especially issueA, issueB, and issueC, while conceding issueD to valueD since issueD has low importance for me and the opponent has recently included valueD there.

At t=0.24 it is still early enough to target a high utility around 0.88 rather than matching the opponent’s recent low-utility offers. The opponent has not shown consistent concession toward my preferences, so I am holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while offering a compromise on issueC and issueD that has appeared in prior bids.

I am targeting a high utility bid of about 0.92 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.26 and the opponent’s offers remain low for me without a clear concession trend. Their bids often include issueD:valueD, so I concede on the lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility bid (about 0.836), since we are still relatively early at t=0.28 and the opponent’s offers have not shown a reliable upward concession trend in my utility. Their latest bid improves issueA to valueB but remains low overall, so I am making a measured concession on issueD, my lowest-weight issue, and on issueC to valueA, which they have previously offered, while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.30, so there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have not meaningfully improved for me, staying around the mid-0.4 utility range, but they often put issueD at valueD, so I concede there while holding firm on the high-weight issues.

At t=0.32 I am still targeting a high-utility bid, but with a modest concession from my maximum to improve acceptability. The opponent’s valid offers have hovered around moderate utility for me and recently include issueA=valueB, issueB=valueC, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueD, so I will mainly concede on lower-cost issueD and partially on issueC while holding firm on my highest-weight priorities issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.73, conceding on issueA and issueD while holding the opponent’s latest concession on issueB=valueA and keeping issueC at my second-best value. At t=0.34 there is time left, but the opponent’s latest offer improved substantially for me, so a reciprocal but still self-interested compromise is appropriate. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueD=valueC and has previously offered issueA=valueB and issueC=valueA, so this bid uses values they have at least signaled some willingness to consider.

I targeted a utility of about 0.62, making a meaningful mid-game concession while still protecting the high-weight issueB at valueA. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueC toward valueC/valueD and issueD toward valueD, and their latest offer included issueA: valueB, so I am conceding on those areas rather than repeating my prior bid.

I targeted a utility around 0.77, which is still strong for me but concedes on issueA and issueD where the opponent has repeatedly pushed away from my top values. Since we are still before the midpoint, I am not ready to accept their latest low-utility offer, but I am offering a credible tradeoff by holding firm on my high-value issueB and issueC.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a moderate concession while keeping utility near 0.69, since there is still time but we should move beyond maximal bids. The opponent’s recent offers repeatedly use issueA=valueB and issueD=valueD, so I concede those, while holding issueB=valueA because it is high-value for me and they have offered it once before; issueC=valueA is a compromise below my top but better than their frequent low-value issueC choices.

I targeted a moderate concession around 0.62 utility, keeping my high-value issueB at valueA while conceding on issueC and issueD where the opponent has repeatedly pushed toward valueC/valueD. At t=0.42 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their latest 0.575-equivalent offer outright; their recent move to issueB: valueA suggests room for a mutually acceptable tradeoff.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66, conceding on issueA and issueC while holding issueB at my top value because issueB is high-weight and the opponent has already offered valueA there. The opponent’s recent bids show movement on issueB but continued pressure on issueA/issueC, so this offer uses issueA:valueB and issueC:valueC as tradeoffs without dropping too close to reservation value at t=0.44.
